untranslatable_reason: Signal requires point-and-figure chart logic (tracking ascending/descending columns of Xs and Os with configurable box sizes). This is a chart pattern specific to P&F analysis. The closest approximation uses Donchian channels to detect breakouts above/below recent highs/lows, but this does not capture the true P&F column structure, reversal logic, or box-size sensitivity. Authentic implementation requires P&F-specific indicators not available in the standard indicator set.
translation_notes: KAUFMAN_DRY_5 is a point-and-figure chart pattern signal. Attempted proxy using dc_upper (recent 20-bar high) for 'highest X of last ascending column' and dc_lower (recent 20-bar low) for 'lowest O of last descending column'. However, P&F analysis fundamentally differs from price-bar analysis: it uses fixed box sizes to filter noise and tracks column direction changes. A proper backtest requires a P&F-specific indicator or custom column-tracking logic. The box_size parameter controls sensitivity but has no direct mapping to standard indicators.
